constraints_1_content: Before writing any script, use `list_dir` to explore the /app/data directory and confirm the exact filenames and paths of vulnerability_db.json and security_policy.json exist, rather than assuming their locations.
constraints_1_rationale: Directly tied to the specific data files in this task; uses framework-specific list_dir tool.
constraints_2_content: Use `view_files` to read and inspect the full content of /app/data/vulnerability_db.json and /app/data/security_policy.json before writing any parsing logic, so the actual schema and field names are confirmed rather than assumed.
constraints_2_rationale: Task-specific because it targets these two data files; framework-dependent because it references the view_files tool.
constraints_3_content: After creating each .sh script, run it with `run_command` to verify it executes without errors and produces the expected output files (security_report.json, remediation_plan.txt) before marking the task complete.
constraints_3_rationale: References specific output files from this task; uses the framework-specific run_command tool.
constraints_4_content: Use `run_command` to execute `pip list --format=json` in a test run and inspect its actual output format before writing the parsing logic in vulnerability_scanner.sh, ensuring the field names match exactly.
constraints_4_rationale: Specific to the pip-parsing logic in this task; uses framework-specific run_command.
constraints_5_content: Both shell scripts must include a shebang line (`#!/usr/bin/env bash`), use `set -euo pipefail` at the top, and include inline comments for each major section (scanning, parsing, report generation).
constraints_5_rationale: Shell scripting best practices specific to the bash scripts in this task; no framework tools referenced.
constraints_6_content: Do not hardcode any file paths inside the scripts beyond what is given in the task (e.g., /app/data/vulnerability_db.json). Use shell variables defined at the top of each script for all configurable paths.
constraints_6_rationale: Specific to the path-heavy nature of this task; no framework tool referenced.
constraints_7_content: When generating security_report.json, use `jq` for JSON construction rather than raw string concatenation or heredocs, to ensure valid JSON output even when package names or descriptions contain special characters.
constraints_7_rationale: Specific to the JSON generation requirement of this task; applies to any environment without referencing framework tools.
constraints_9_content: If `jq` is not available in the environment, verify this with `run_command` (`which jq`) before falling back to Python-based JSON processing via `python3 -c`; do NOT use awk or sed for JSON manipulation.
constraints_9_rationale: Specific to the JSON-heavy scripts in this task; references run_command for environment checking.
constraints_10_content: All error messages in both scripts must be written to stderr (>&2), and success/info messages to stdout, so that output file content is never polluted with diagnostic text.
constraints_10_rationale: Relevant to the scripts that produce structured output files; no framework tool dependency.
